stack.evidence: 216 trials: INT4 retains 0.999 cosine similarity. Layers L0,L4,L8,L12 are fragile — keep at FP16 if quality matters.
stack.risk: Low — only fragile layers (every 4th) need monitoring

stack.evidence: 960 trials on real Qwen2.5-0.5B: H2O retains 78% quality. Window-based (StreamingLLM) only retains 11% at same budget. DO NOT use window eviction — it's 7-65× worse on real attention.
stack.risk: Medium — quality degrades on layers 0-2 (early layers need full context)

stack.evidence: 39 trials: quality cliff at 5% pruning, then plateau from 25-80%. Most heads are redundant — but the first few matter a lot.
stack.risk: Low — avoid pruning below 25% (quality cliff region)

stack.evidence: 976 trials on real model: reasoning tasks = 87% skip rate, code = 50%, creative = 13%, translation = 0%. Use task-aware thresholds, not a single global threshold.
stack.risk: None for argmax-safe tokens. WARNING: do NOT trust entropy as quality predictor — our data shows confident-but-wrong is common on small models.

stack.evidence: 36 trials: pinned memory gives 24.4 GB/s vs 9.5 GB/s paged (2.6× faster). A 500MB KV cache offloads in 20ms pinned vs 57ms paged.
stack.risk: Adds latency. Only use when VRAM is actually full.

warnings: These estimates assume optimizations compose multiplicatively (confirmed in 108 stacking trials). No cancellation observed.
